response.world_assessment: The dominant macro event this week is Trump's 'Liberation Day' tariff announcement (April 2), which analyst Dan Ives describes as creating 'major uncertainty' for Big Tech and broad markets. This is a regime-changing moment: companies with heavy Asia/China supply chain exposure (tech hardware, apparel) face serious margin compression, while domestic energy producers, defensive healthcare, and value retailers are relative beneficiaries. Taiwan Semiconductor is already down 15% YTD with investors worried about its US expansion plans amid tariff crossfire. The market is broadly weak — most stocks below SMA50 — but defensives and energy are holding up with strong technicals. This is NOT a time to deploy aggressively into growth; it's a time to be selective, own domestic earnings power, and short China-exposed supply chains.

lessons: When holding a short position within 5% of its stop price heading into a scheduled earnings event, the gap risk must be explicitly assessed. If the earnings report could plausibly show turnaround progress (as with SBUX Q2), the position is inadequately protected — the stock can gap through the stop before it can be executed. Action: when a short is within 5% of its stop and earnings are within 7 days, either (1) tighten the stop to create more buffer on the other side, or (2) reduce position size by 50% pre-earnings and restore it post-event if the thesis remains intact. Trigger: any short position within 5% of its stop price when a known earnings date falls within the next 7 days.

belief_updates.name: Disciplined Capital Deployment
belief_updates.description: Never fully deploy capital in the first review of a new macro regime. Maintain a substantial cash buffer (40%+) in the early weeks to add to winners as theses prove out, capitalize on pullbacks in high-conviction names, and open positions as the macro picture clarifies. The opportunity cost of excess cash is far lower than the cost of full deployment when uncertainty is highest. In a fast-moving macro environment, flexibility is alpha.

new_positions.thesis: Home Depot faces structural headwinds from the high-rate environment suppressing housing turnover and discretionary home improvement spending. With mortgage rates elevated, housing mobility is near-frozen — homeowners stay put rather than trigger new mortgages, directly suppressing both the home purchase pipeline and the renovation spending that follows a move. The same Consumer Downgrade dynamic that benefits DG and COST works directly against HD — the budget-constrained consumer delays $5,000-50,000 home improvement projects in favor of essentials.
